title: Overflow

9.name: Buffer Overflow
9.vuln: Can be exploited to cause unexpected behavior, useful for many attack vectors.

18.name: Format String Vulnerability
18.vuln: Allows leaking stack data or writing to memory via format specifiers.

title: Kernel

18.name: Integer Underflow
18.vuln: If 'len' is 0, 'len - 1' wraps to the maximum value of size_t, bypassing the bounds check entirely.

22.name: Heap/Mmap Buffer Overflow
22.vuln: Reading 'len + 16' bytes into a buffer of size 'len' allows overwriting adjacent memory metadata or mapped structures.

29.name: Use-After-Free (UAF)
29.vuln: The 'session' object is freed on a specific 'req_type' but the callback is still invoked, allowing an attacker to hijack the instruction pointer (EIP/RIP) if they can reclaim that heap chunk.
